channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In Mexico, Juneteenth has been celebrated for generations
item.description: Juneteenth is often told as an American story. But it's been celebrated for generations in Corina Torralba Harrington's hometown in Mexico by descendants of Black Seminoles.
item.content:encoded: Juneteenth is often told as an American story. But it's been celebrated for generations in Corina Torralba Harrington's hometown in Mexico by descendants of Black Seminoles.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Why we're still obsessed with aliens on screen
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: What's in a name? The origin of the 'Knick'
item.description: What is a Knickerbocker? NPR's Elissa Nadworny talks to Peter-Christian Aigner, Director of the Gotham Center, to find out.
item.content:encoded: What is a Knickerbocker? NPR's Elissa Nadworny talks to Peter-Christian Aigner, Director of the Gotham Center, to find out.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: World Capitals
item.description: NPR's David Folkenflik plays the puzzle with Vermont Public listener Judy Alexander of South Burlington, Vermont along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's David Folkenflik plays the puzzle with Vermont Public listener Judy Alexander of South Burlington, Vermont along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for June 13, 2026: With Not My Job guest Robert Smigel
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, special guest Robert Smigel and panelists Josh Gondelman, Shantira Jackson, and Shane Torres
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, special guest Robert Smigel and panelists Josh Gondelman, Shantira Jackson, and Shane Torres

(Image credit: Jamie McCarthy)

item.title: William Sutton shares his rare achievement of memorizing all 154 Shakespeare's sonnets
item.description: Modern life has fried our attention spans. Could flexing our long term memory muscles help? NPR's Elissa Nadworny talks to actor William Sutton, who knows all 154 Shakespearian sonnets by heart.
item.content:encoded: Modern life has fried our attention spans. Could flexing our long term memory muscles help? NPR's Elissa Nadworny talks to actor William Sutton, who knows all 154 Shakespearian sonnets by heart.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: Tessa Yang talks about her debut novel, 'The Jellyfish Problem'
item.description: A stay-out-of-the-water beach read features a giant, sentient sea creature. NPR's Elissa Nadworny talks with Tessa Yang about her debut novel, "The Jelly Fish Problem."
item.content:encoded: A stay-out-of-the-water beach read features a giant, sentient sea creature. NPR's Elissa Nadworny talks with Tessa Yang about her debut novel, "The Jelly Fish Problem."
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: 'Cool Ladies Club' is directed by 10 working-class women. They live up to the title
item.description: They gave smartphones to 10 women from a working-class Indian community to make a documentary about their unseen and unheralded lives. The results are .... pretty cool.
item.content:encoded:
They gave smartphones to 10 women from a working-class Indian community to make a documentary about their unseen and unheralded lives. The results are .... pretty cool.

(Image credit: Mangesh Gudekar/School of Media and Cultural Studies, TISS.
)
item.dc:creator: Neha Bhatt

item.title: Gene Shalit, longtime 'Today' show movie critic, dies at 100
item.description: Known for his puffy hair, oversized handlebar mustache and a love for puns, Gene Shalit joined Today in 1970 and became arts editor in 1973. He was a middle-of-the-road critic, known for his wit and intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
Known for his puffy hair, oversized handlebar mustache and a love for puns, Gene Shalit joined Today in 1970 and became arts editor in 1973. He was a middle-of-the-road critic, known for his wit and intelligence.

(Image credit: Richard Drew)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Kennedy Center removes Trump's name from the building
item.description: Workers finished removing President Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday, hours after a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.
item.content:encoded:
Workers finished removing President Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday, hours after a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab
